WAEC_English_Language_Objective_Question.title: WAEC English Language Objective Test Questions (Complete Merged List)
WAEC_English_Language_Objective_Question.instruction: The following is a complete list of the WAEC English Language objective questions (1-82/90) including Vocabulary, Structure, Cloze Passage, and Test of Orals, organized with sequential numbering, answers, and brief explanations.

questions.question: The hope with which he assumed office contrasts with the ________ which he is leaving.
questions.explanation: The direct opposite of 'hope' is 'despair'.

questions.question: The lies she told will merely exacerbate the situation rather than ________ it.
questions.explanation: To 'exacerbate' is to make worse; 'improve' is the opposite.

questions.question: You should not say things that will enrage the children rather than ________ them.
questions.explanation: 'Pacify' (to calm) is the opposite of 'enrage'.

questions.question: The doctor does not think the patient will recover; the signs are ominous rather than ________.
questions.explanation: 'Ominous' means threatening; 'encouraging' means promising.

questions.question: School prefects are expected to be honorable in all their conduct, not ________.
questions.explanation: 'Devious' (dishonest) is the opposite of 'honorable'.

questions.question: Those who praised the king were rewarded while those who ________ him were punished.
questions.explanation: 'Vilified' (to speak ill of) is the opposite of 'praised'.

questions.question: We had expected the change of colour to be ________ but it was imperceptible.
questions.explanation: 'Obvious' is the opposite of 'imperceptible' (unnoticeable).

questions.question: We need to teach children to be ________ not insolent.
questions.explanation: 'Insolent' means rude; 'respectful' is the opposite.

questions.question: I prefer the hustle of the city life to the ________ of the village.
questions.explanation: 'Tranquility' (peace) contrasts with 'hustle' (noise/activity).

questions.question: The soldiers had to take a split decision whether to advance or ________.
questions.explanation: 'Retreat' is the military opposite of 'advance'.

questions.question: The teacher reached the ________ of his career before he retired.
questions.explanation: 'Peak' refers to the height of a professional career.

questions.question: There will be a yearly ________ to determine each employee's contribution.
questions.explanation: 'Assessment' is the evaluation of work performance.

questions.question: The man was honoured for fighting ________ in the war.
questions.explanation: 'Gallantly' implies bravery and honor in battle.

questions.question: The new manager did not ________ responsibilities because she felt she can do everything alone.
questions.explanation: To 'delegate' means to entrust a task to another person.

questions.question: The ________ of a higher turnover next year may be affected by inflation.
questions.explanation: A 'forecast' is a prediction of future trends.

questions.question: The revelations rendered her ________ for reelection questionable.
questions.explanation: 'Eligibility' refers to the legal right to be elected.

questions.question: We need you to be the ________ in case the actor is unable to make it.
questions.explanation: An 'understudy' is a standby performer.

questions.question: The doctor warned the patient might develop an ________ to Septrin.
questions.explanation: An 'allergy' is a specific immune reaction to medication.

questions.question: Some students are fond of ________ away their money on frivolous things.
questions.explanation: 'Frittering' means wasting time or money on trifles.

questions.question: Mountainous regions are prone to volcanic ________.
questions.explanation: 'Eruption' is the standard term for volcanoes.

questions.question: As a school prefect, I must be 'whiter than white'. This means:
questions.explanation: 'Whiter than white' means to be completely honest and beyond reproach.

questions.question: Not to 'let the grass grow under his feet' means he should:
questions.explanation: This idiom means to take action immediately.

questions.question: Had he confessed much earlier, she would have trusted him. This means:

options.B: He didn't confess and wasn't trusted
options.D: He didn't confess but was trusted

questions.explanation: The past conditional implies the action (confessing) never happened.

questions.question: Investing in the business is a 'long shot'. This means it:
questions.explanation: A 'long shot' is an attempt that has a very small chance of working.

questions.question: Woke up at the 'crack of dawn'. This means:
questions.explanation: The very first light of the morning.

questions.question: The rector is in the 'hot seat'. This means he:
questions.explanation: The 'hot seat' is a position of high pressure and scrutiny.

questions.question: There was 'little food' for him at home. This means:
questions.explanation: 'Little' without 'a' means almost none.

questions.question: The manager is 'on the ball'. This means he is:
questions.explanation: 'On the ball' means alert and efficient.

questions.question: 'Cutting off his nose to spite his face' means the decision:
questions.explanation: This idiom refers to hurting oneself while trying to punish another.

questions.question: A 'stay of execution' means the action against the company should be:
questions.explanation: A 'stay' is a temporary suspension of a legal order.

questions.question: Extended the frontiers of knowledge.
questions.explanation: 'Frontiers' and 'borders' are synonyms for the limits of knowledge.

questions.question: Music is inappropriate for such a solemn ceremony.
questions.explanation: 'Inappropriate' means unsuitable.

questions.question: Solitude is not good for the aged.
questions.explanation: 'Solitude' means being alone or in seclusion.

questions.question: The recalcitrant was not appointed prefect.
questions.explanation: 'Recalcitrant' refers to someone who is unruly or disobedient.

questions.question: The professor's explanation was incisive.
questions.explanation: 'Incisive' means clear and sharp.

questions.question: His father is jocular.
questions.explanation: 'Jocular' relates to being humorous or hilarious.

questions.question: The electorate misconstructed the intention.
questions.explanation: To 'misconstruct' is to misinterpret.

questions.question: Suggestions were absurd.
questions.explanation: 'Absurd' means ridiculous.

questions.question: Ann's behaviour has been weird.
questions.explanation: 'Weird' means strange.

questions.question: Exhibit some amount of decorum.
questions.explanation: 'Decorum' is decency or proper behavior.

questions.question: ________ football, I also play basketball.
questions.explanation: 'Besides' means 'in addition to'.

questions.question: Allowed to carry ________ on this flight.
questions.explanation: 'Luggage' is uncountable; use 'much'.

questions.question: The class visited the apiary ________.
questions.explanation: 'Ago' marks a point in the past.

questions.question: If she had prepared well... she ________ won the scholarship.
questions.explanation: Third conditional requires 'would have'.

questions.question: Peter and Paul come often, ________?
questions.explanation: Positive statement in present tense uses 'don't they'.

questions.question: My family ________ Freedom Square twice in the 1990s.
questions.explanation: Simple past for a completed historical action.

questions.question: The ________ who attended appreciated the talk.
questions.explanation: Pluralize the head noun 'father'.

questions.question: We videoed him ________ the phone.
questions.explanation: Continuous participle describing the observed action.

questions.question: Put the ________ on the rack.
questions.explanation: Order: Size, then Color, then Material.

questions.question: Tolu is the ________ girl in her class.
questions.explanation: Single superlative 'cleverest' is correct.

questions.question: You won't tell him the answer, ________?
questions.explanation: Negative 'won't' takes positive 'will you'.

questions.question: The most foolish decision ________.
questions.explanation: Perfect tense for lifetime experience.

questions.question: I only flick ________ newspapers.
questions.explanation: 'Flick through' means to look through quickly.

questions.question: Ensure you ________ my office.
questions.explanation: 'Call at' means to visit a place.

questions.question: There was ________ than expected.
questions.explanation: 'Much more' is correct for uncountable comparison.

questions.question: The baby became happy when ________ mother returned.
questions.explanation: 'Its' is used when the gender of the baby is unknown.

questions.question: The faster you work ________ you will finish.
questions.explanation: Double comparative structure 'the... the...'.

questions.question: We don't discuss... because she is ________.
questions.explanation: 'Talkative' is an adjective, modified by 'very'.

questions.question: I'm sure you know ________ that she is generous.
questions.explanation: 'As well as I do' is the correct comparison of manner.

questions.question: They are all rooting ________ Adamu.
questions.explanation: 'Root for' means to support.

questions.question: Your eyes are puffy ________?
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous for a past action with present results.

questions.question: His mother never ________ him.
questions.explanation: 'Give up on' means to lose faith in someone.

questions.question: That is the boy to ________ many awards were given.
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is used after a preposition.

questions.question: The dancing ________ the party.
questions.explanation: 'Liven up' means to make more lively.

questions.question: Punished them with a view ________ them.
questions.explanation: 'With a view to' is followed by the -ing form.

questions.question: He spends a ________ deal of time.
questions.explanation: Standard idiom: 'A great deal'.

questions.question: The champion has been ________.

options.B: very much off-form most of the time

questions.explanation: Correct order of adverbial frequency and manner.

questions.question: Labourers work on ________ days.
questions.explanation: 'Alternate' means every other day.

questions.question: Akwu reasons ________.
questions.explanation: Adverb modifying an adverb.

questions.question: Found the movie ________ boring.
questions.explanation: 'Absolutely' emphasizes the intensity of the adjective.

questions.question: The [71] was filled to capacity.
questions.explanation: Location of the match.

questions.question: The [72] were singing.
questions.explanation: People watching a sports event.

questions.question: The two [73] filled in.
questions.explanation: Participants in the match.

questions.question: Led by [74] and assistant.
questions.explanation: The main official on the field.

questions.question: Their [75] cheered.
questions.explanation: The fans of the teams.

questions.question: Committed any [76].
questions.explanation: A breach of football rules.

questions.question: Bad [77] could show red card.
questions.explanation: A dangerous move during play.

questions.question: There was a match [78].
questions.explanation: The person overseeing match rules/conduct.

questions.question: End of [79] time.
questions.explanation: Standard match duration.

questions.question: Neither team had [80].
questions.explanation: Neither team had scored or allowed a goal.
